hadiths.ar:
1123  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ ج َّمِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ ذَّر َّإح ٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ إ ِمَّامِ ق َّوْمٍ إ َّج ْن َّث َّ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ مَّعَّهُّ مِن َّ إَّلْمَّاءِ مَّا ي َّكْق ِي هِ 
لِلْع ُّسْلِ وَّ مَّعَّهُّمْ مَّاء  ي َّي َّوَّص  َّن ُّون َّ ن ِهِ ق َّي َّي َّوَّص  َّا ُّ ن َّعْص ُّهُّمْ وَّ ي َّو ُّم ُّهُّمْ ق َّال أ ِمَّامُّ وَّ ي َّو ُّم ُّهُّمْ إ ِن  َّ إَّلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ  
ج َّل َّ ج َّ عَّلَّ إَّلْأ َّرْض َّ طَّهُّورإً كَّمَّا ج َّعَّلَّ إَّلْمَّاءَّ طَّهُّورإً .
hadiths.en:
Imam Ali {a.s} said regarding two men who disagreed, with one saying, "I was 
your Imam (leader in prayer)," and the other saying, "I was your Imam," that " are their 
prayers are valid?."  
When asked what if one of them said: "I was following you (as the Imam)," and the 
other also said: "I was following you,"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Then their prayers are invalid, and they must repeat the prayer”.  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 375 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 382 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 3, p. 54 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1277 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 352 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 6, p. 477 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1122
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٢٢

hadiths.ar:
1124 - وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْهُّ عُّمَّزُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّز ِي ذَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ى  مَّعَّهُّمْ صَّلاَّة ً 
ي َّق ِي  َّه ً وَّ هُّوَّ مُّي َّوَّص ِّى   إ ِلأ َّ كَّي َّث َّ إَّلل َّهُّ لَّهُّ ن ِهَّا ج َّمْساً وَّ عِس ْزِي ن َّ ذَّرَّج َّه ً ق َّارْع َّن ُّوإ ق ِى  ذ َّلِك َّ .
hadiths.en:
Jamil ibn Darraj asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} about an Imam 
who becomes ritually impure (junub) and does not have enough water to perform the 
full ritual bath (ghusl), while the rest of the congregation has water to perform ablution 
(wudu). He asked whether some of them should perform wudu and then a llow the 
Imam to lead.  
Abu Abdullah {a.s} replied: "No, but the Imam should perform tayammum (dry ablution) 
and lead them, for Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, has made the earth a purifier 
just as He has made water a purifier”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1123
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٢٣

hadiths.ar:
1125  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْهُّ جَّم َّاذُّ ن ْن ُّ عُّي ْمَّان َّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال لْف َّ رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ  
صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ق ِى  إَّلص َّف ِّ إَّلْأ َّو َّلِ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from him by Umar ibn Yazid that Imam {a.s} said:  
"None of you prays an obligatory prayer at its prescribed time and then prays with them 
(in congregation) as an act of precaution (taqiyya) while in a state of wudu, except that 
Allah {SWT} will record for him twenty-five degrees (of reward). So strive for this." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 382 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1218 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 302 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1124
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٢٤

hadiths.en:
It is narrated from him by Hammad ibn Uthman that Imam {a.s} said:  
"Whoever prays with them in the first row, it is as if he prayed behind the Messenger of 
Allah {saws} in the first row”. 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 380 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 382 • Al -Arba‘ūn Ḥadīthan 
(Al-Shahīd al-Awwal) Vol. 1, p. 77 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1218 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1218 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 299 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 300 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 98 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1125
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٢٥

hadiths.ar:
1127  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مَّسْعَّذَّة ُّ ن ْن ُّ صَّذَّق َّه :  إ َّن  َّ ق َّان ِلاً ق َّالَّ لِح َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ج ُّعِلْث ُّ ق ِذَّإك َّ إ ِن ِّى  إ َّمُّز ُّ ن ِق َّوْمٍ ن َّاصِن ِي  َّه ٍ 
وَّ ق َّذْ إ ُّق ِي مَّث ْ لَّهُّمُّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ُّ وَّ إ َّن َّا عَّلَّى ع َّي ْزِ وُّص ُّوءٍ ق َّا ِن ْ لَّمْ إ َّذْج ُّلْ مَّعَّهُّمْ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ ق َّالُّوإ مَّا  س َّاءُّوإ  إ َّن ْ ي َّق ُّولُّوإ إ َّ ق َّا ُّصَّلِّى   
مَّعَّهُّمْ ي ُّم َّ إ َّي َّوَّص  َّا ُّ إ ِذ َّإ إِن ْصَّزَّق ْث ُّ وَّ إ ُّصَّلِّى  ق َّالَّ ج َّعْق َّزُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم هِ إ َّ ق َّمَّا ي َّح َّاف ُّ مَّن ْ ي ُّصَّلِّى   
عَّلَّى ع َّي ْزِ وُّص ُّوءٍ إ َّن ْ ي َّا ْج ُّذ َّةُّ إَّلْأ َّرْض ُّ ج َّسْق اً .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from him by Hafs ibn al-Bakhtari that Imam {a.s} said: 
"It will be counted for you when you join them (in prayer), even if you do not follow them 
(in intention); it will be counted for you just as it would if you were with someone you 
follow”. 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 373 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 383 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 3, p. 265 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1217 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 299 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1126
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٢٦

hadiths.ar:
1128  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ر َّي ْذ  إَّلس  َّح َّامُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ر َّي ْذُّ ج َّالِق ُّوإ إَّلن  َّاسَّ ن ِا َّج ْلاَّق ِهِمْ صَّل ُّوإ ق ِى  مَّسَّاج ِذِهِمْ وَّ 
عُّوذُّوإ مَّزْص َّاهُّمْ وَّ إِس ْهَّذُّوإ ج َّن َّان ِز َّهُّمْ وَّ إ ِن ِ إِسْي َّطَّعْي ُّمْ إ َّن ْ ي َّكُّون ُّوإ إَّلْأ َّن ِم َّه َّ وَّ إَّلْمُّو َّذ ِّن ِي ن َّ ق َّاق ْ عَّلُّوإ ق َّا ِن  َّكُّمْ إ ِذ َّإ ق َّعَّلْي ُّمْ ذ َّلِك َّ  
ق َّالُّوإ هَّو ُّلأَّءِ  إَّلْح َّعْق َّزِي  َّه ُّ رَّجِمَّ إَّلل َّهُّ ج َّعْق َّزإً مَّا كَّان َّ إ َّجْسَّن َّ مَّا ي ُّو َّذِّب ُّ إ َّصْحَّان َّهُّ وَّ إ ِذ َّإ ي َّزَّكْي ُّمْ ذ َّلِك َّ ق َّالُّوإ   هَّو ُّلأَّءِ إَّلْح َّعْق َّزِي  َّه ُّ 
ق َّعَّلَّ إَّلل َّهُّ ن ِح َّعْق َّزٍ مَّا كَّان َّ إ َّسْوَّإ َّ مَّا ي ُّو َّذِّب ُّ إ َّصْحَّان َّهُّ .
hadiths.en:
Mas’adah ibn Sadaqah narrates that someone said to Ja far ibn Muhammad 
{a.s}:  
"May I be your ransom! I pass by a group of Nasibis (those hostile to the AhlulBayt {a.s}), 
and their prayer has been established while I am not in a state of wudu (ritual purity). 
If I do not join them in prayer, they will say whatever they wish about me. Should I then 
pray with them and perform wudu afterward and pray again?"  
Jafar ibn Muhammad {a.s} responded: "Glory be to Allah {SWT}! Does he not fear that if he 
prays without wudu, the earth would swallow him up?"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 383 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 7, p. 43 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 1, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1127
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٢٧

hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Zayd al -Shahham that He said: Imam Ja'far Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
said:  
"O’ Zayd, interact with the people through good character. Pray in their mosques, visit 
their sick, and attend their funerals. If you can, be the Imams and callers to prayer 
(mu'adhdhins), then do so. For if you do this, they will say,  
'These are the Ja'fariyyah; may Allah {SWT} have mercy on Ja'far, how well he trained his 
companions!'  
But if you do otherwise, they will say,  
'These are the Ja'fariyyah; may Allah {SWT} do with Ja'far as he deserves for how badly he 
trained his companions!'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 383 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1220 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 430 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1128
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٢٨

hadiths.ar:
1130  -  وَّق َّال لَّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ رَّج ُّل  إ ُّصَّلِّى  ق ِى  إ َّهْلِى   ي ُّم َّ إ َّج ْزُّح ُّ إ ِلَّى إَّلْمَّسْح ِذِ ق َّي ُّق َّذِّمُّون ِى  ق َّق َّال 
صَّلِّ ن ِهِمْ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Give the call to prayer (adhan) behind the one you recited the prayer behind”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 383 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 56 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, 
p. 1209 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 443 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 365 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1129
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٢٩

hadiths.ar: 1131  -  وَّ رَّوَّى هِس َّامُّ ن ْن ُّ سَّالِمٍ عَّن ْهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: ق ِى  إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّصَّلِّى  إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ وَّجْذَّةُّ ي ُّم َّ ي ح ِذُّ ج َّمَّاعَّه ً ق َّالَّ
hadiths.en:
A man said to him {a.s}:  
"I pray with my family, then I go to the mosque and they ask me to lead them in prayer."  
Imam {a.s} said: "Go ahead and lead them; there is no harm in it. Pray with them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 383 • Al-Wafi, V. 8, p. 1218 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
« .

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1130

reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٣٠

hadiths.en:
Hisham ibn Salim narrated from him {a.s} that He said regarding a man who 
prays alone and then finds a congregation:  
"He may pray with them and count it as his obligatory (fardh) prayer if he wishes”.  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 379 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 383 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 3, p. 50 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1247 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 401 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 403 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1131
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٣١

hadiths.ar:
1133  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ إ َّج َّاةُّ مُّوسَّى ن ْن َّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ هَّلْ ي ُّصَّلِّى  ن ِالْق َّوْمِ وَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ سَّزَّإوِي لُّ  
وَّ رِذَّإء  ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated: "He will be credited with the better and more complete 
of the two”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1132
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٣٢

hadiths.ar:
1134  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّه ق َّال 
إ لِهِ ن ِالن  َّاسِ ق ِى  ي َّوْب ٍ وَّإجِذٍ ق َّذْ ج َّالَّف َّ ن َّي ْن َّ طَّزَّق َّي ْهِ إ َّ لأَّ إ ُّرِي ك َّ إَّلي  َّوْب لْحَّق َّه ً ق َّذ َّرَّعْي ُّهَّا وَّ كَّان َّث ْ  
سَّن ْعَّه َّ إ َّذ ْ رُّعٍ ق ِى  ي َّمَّان ِي َّه ِ إ َّس ْن َّارٍ .
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s} about a 
man leading a group in prayer while wearing trousers (sarawil) and a cloak (rida).  
He {a.s} replied: "There is no harm in it”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1133
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٣٣

hadiths.ar:
1135  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عُّمَّزُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّز ِي ذَّ ، إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلزِّوَّإي َّه ِ إَّل َّي ِى  ي َّزْوُّون َّ إ َّن  َّهُّ لأَّ ي َّن ْن َّع ِى  إ َّن ْ ي ُّي َّطَّو َّعَّ ق ِى   
وَّق ْث ِ كُّلِّ ق َّزِي ص َّه ٍ مَّا جَّذ ُّ هَّذ َّإ إَّلْوَّق ْث ِ ق َّق َّال ن  َّاسَّ ي َّح ْي َّلِق ُّون َّ ق ِى  إَّلْأ ِق َّامَّه ِ  
ق َّال مُّق ِي مُّ إَّل َّذ ِى  ي ُّصَّل َّى مَّعَّهُّ .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from Imam Al -Baqir {a.s} that He said: "The last prayer that 
the Messenger of Allah {saws} led the people in was while wearing a single garment, with 
its ends crossed over one another. Shall I show you the garment?" I said: "Yes."  
He then brought out a cloak, and I measured it - it was seven cubits by eight hand 
spans. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 384 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 4, p. 391 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1134
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٣٤

hadiths.ar:
1136 - وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ جَّق ْضُّ ن ْن ُّ سَّالِمٍ : إ ِذ َّإ ق َّالَّ إَّلْمُّو َّذ ِّن ُّ ق َّذْ ق َّامَّث ِ إَّلص َّلاَّة ُّ إ َّ ي َّق ُّومُّ إَّلن  َّاسُّ عَّلَّى إ َّرْج ُّلِهِمْ إ َّوْ ي َّح ْلِسُّون َّ جَّي  َّى  
ي َّح ِى ءَّ إ ِمَّامُّهُّمْ ق َّال لٍ مِن َّ إَّلْق َّوْ مِ ق َّي ُّق َّذ َّمَّ .
hadiths.en:
Umar ibn Yazid asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about the 
narration that states it is not appropriate to perform supererogatory (nafl) prayers at 
the time of every obligatory (fardh) prayer.  
He asked: "What is the limit for this time?"  
He {a.s} replied: "When the person giving the iqamah (call immediately preceding the 
prayer) begins the iqamah."  
Umar said: "People differ in the timing of the iqamah."  
He {a.s} responded: "The one giving the iqamah is the one with whom the prayer is being 
established”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 384 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 283 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1135
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٣٥

hadiths.ar:
1137  -  وروى ر رإرة  عن  إ ن ى  ج عق ز علي ه إلسلام إ ن ه ق ال: " إ ذ إ إ ق ي مث  إلصلاة  جزم إلكلام على إلأمام وإ هل 
إلمسح ذ إ لأ ق ى  ي ق ذي م إ مام .
hadiths.en:
Hafs ibn Salim asked: "When the muezzin (caller to prayer) says 'The prayer 
has been established' (during the iqamah), should the people stand up or remain 
seated until their Imam arrives?"  
He {a.s} replied: "No, they should stand up. If their Imam arrives, they proceed with him; 
otherwise, a man from among the congregation should be taken by the hand and 
brought forward to lead." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1136
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٣٦

hadiths.en:
Zurarah narrated from Imam Al -Baqir {a.s} that He said: "When the prayer has 
been established (by the iqamah), speaking becomes forbidden for the Imam and the 
people of the mosque, except in the matter of appointing an Imam”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1137
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٣٧

hadiths.ar:
1139  -  ق َّال وَّق َّالَّ  رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :  «
ق ُّذ َّإمِى  وَّ مِن ْ ن َّي ْن ِ ي َّذَّى  َّ وَّ لأَّ ي ُّح َّالِق ُّوإ ق َّي ُّح َّالِف َّ إَّلل َّهُّ ن َّي ْن َّ ق ُّلُّون ِكُّمْ .
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim that he was asked about a man 
leading two others in prayer.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should stand in front of them and not between them."  
And regarding two men praying together in congregation, Imam {a.s} said:  
"Yes, he (the one leading) should have the other stand on his right”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 385 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 342 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1138
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٣٨

hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said:  
"Straighten your rows, for I see you from behind me just as I see you from in front of me 
and from before me. Do not differ, lest Allah {SWT} cause discord among your hearts." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 385 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 423 • Bihar Al Anwar, 
Vol. 85, p. 74 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 6, p. 505 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1139
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٣٩

hadiths.ar: 1141  - وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ن ِالص ُّق ُّوف ِ ن َّي ْن َّ إَّلْأ َّسَّاطِي ن ِ ن َّا ْساً .
hadiths.en:
Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s} said:  
"Praying in the first row is like striving (jihad) in the way of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and 
Majestic." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 385 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1188 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 307 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1140
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٤٠

hadiths.ar:
1142  -  وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم رَّإ َّي ْي ُّمْ ج َّلَّلاً وَّ لأَّ ي َّص ُّز ُّك َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّي َّا َّج  َّزَّ وَّرَّإءَّك َّ إ ِذ َّإ وَّج َّذْب َّ ص ِي ق اً ق ِى   
إَّلص َّف ِّ إَّلْأ َّو َّلِ إ ِلَّى إَّلص َّف ِّ إَّل َّذ ِى  ج َّلْق َّك َّ وَّ ي َّمْس ِى َّ مُّن ْحَّزِق اً .
hadiths.en:
Al-Halabi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"I see no harm in rows (of prayer) being between the columns (pillars)."  
[REFERENCES] Fiqh al-Reza {a.s}, vol. 1, p. 125 • Al -Kafi, Volume 3, p. 386 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 
1, p. 386 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 408 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 6, p. 499 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1141
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٤١
